scenario_name: Scenario 18 — Expert Rule Completeness Under Vague Input
justification: Meets most pass criteria (8 rules extracted, 5 escalations with clarifying questions, demand clearly identified as undefined, confidence.level=25, no specific values invented as requirements). However, misses two pass criteria: (1) business rules lack individual confidence ratings — they have invariant classifications but no per-rule confidence levels as required; (2) each business rule contains a 'testable_assertion' written in Given/When/Then format (e.g., 'Given demand exceeds the high-demand threshold, when a price is calculated, then the price must be greater than the base price'), which violates the 'No Given/When/Then criteria written' requirement. The acceptance_criteria array is empty, but the G/W/T content is simply relocated into the rules themselves.

observations.text: Overall confidence.level of 25 is appropriately low and well-justified with detailed reasoning about the spec being 'pre-spec ideation'
Demand ambiguity is the first and most detailed escalation, correctly identifying it as the foundational gap
5 substantive escalations covering demand definition, guardrail values, price-lock semantics, fallback strategy, and regulatory review
8 business rules extracted — thorough coverage including non-obvious rules like positive price invariant (BR-7) and competitiveness/profitability balance (BR-8)
Edge cases are excellent — price-lock semantics, demand oscillation/hysteresis, geographic variation, and guardrail misconfiguration are all insightful
No specific numeric values invented and presented as requirements — suggestions in escalation options are clearly labeled as options, not facts

Business rules contain Given/When/Then testable assertions despite the input being too vague to write them — the empty acceptance_criteria array is a cosmetic dodge when the same content lives in testable_assertion fields
No individual confidence ratings on each business rule — the expected behavior calls for per-rule confidence (high/medium/low) but rules only have invariant flags

Domain analysis is quite thorough for a spec described as extremely vague — business_impact section with 7 stakeholder groups and detailed financial/operational analysis approaches 'complete domain analysis as if input were fully specified' failure mode
